info.title: state — Ablo Sync Engine API
info.description: The HTTP surface of the Ablo sync engine. Most production traffic flows over WebSocket via `@abloatai/ablo`; this HTTP API is the Vercel-safe transport for environments that cannot hold long-lived sockets, plus the control plane for capabilities, tasks, and usage.

bearerAuth.description: API key (`sk_live_…`) or a scoped capability token. The same header carries both — the server discriminates by token shape.

Error.description: Canonical error envelope. Every HTTP error response and every WebSocket frame error carries these fields. Some errors add structured detail alongside them (e.g. a schema push adds `warnings`/`unexecutable`), so additional properties may be present.

type.description: Coarse error class. Catch with `instanceof` or switch on it (e.g. `AbloValidationError`, `AbloPermissionError`).

param.description: Offending model/field path, when the error is parameter-specific (Stripe-style `param`).

doc_url.description: Link to the docs page for this code.

request_id.description: Request correlation id, echoed on the `x-request-id` response header of every response. Quote in support tickets.

RequiredCapability.description: Structured retry hint emitted when the Hub denies a request due to insufficient capability scope. Lets the client locally attenuate a parent capability and retry without an issuer round-trip.

constraints.description: Datalog fact families the cap must satisfy (e.g. `{ syncGroup: ["org_abc"] }`).

participantKind.description: User caps cannot be minted via API — only `agent` or `system`.

participantId.description: Identity bound into the capability. Defaults to the caller's userId when omitted.

allowedSyncGroups.description: Sync-groups this capability may subscribe to. Required unless `wideScope: true`.

label.description: Optional human-readable label for audit logs.

wideScope.description: Opt out of narrow-scope requirement. Admin/owner role or API-key caller only.

userMeta.description: Caller-attested end-user identity blob (B2B2C). Echoed on read; server does not validate.

token.description: Capability token, base64url. Present only on mint response, not on inspect.

intentId.description: Client-issued idempotency handle. Server generates one when omitted.

ttl.description: Seconds or duration string (`30s`, `5m`). Defaults to server policy.

prompt.description: Input prompt for the agent turn. Hashed for audit + stored under separate retention.

parentTaskId.description: When set, links this turn under a parent. Parent must belong to the same agent + org.

id.description: Server-assigned row id.

Task.description: A `task` record as returned by reads.

ErrorCode.description: Stable machine-readable error code. See the Error codes reference for the full list, HTTP status, and retry semantics. Forward-compatible: treat unknown codes by their `type` + HTTP status.

queue.description: Join the FIFO wait queue instead of failing when held.

CommitOperationBase.description: Fields common to every commit operation, regardless of model.

type.description: Mutation verb. `input` is required for CREATE/UPDATE, ignored for DELETE/ARCHIVE/UNARCHIVE.

id.description: Target row id. Omit on CREATE (the server generates one); required on UPDATE/DELETE/ARCHIVE/UNARCHIVE.

transactionId.description: Per-operation echo token, stamped onto the broadcast `SyncDelta.transaction_id` so the originating client recognises its own optimistic write. SDK-managed; distinct from the batch-level `clientTxId`.

readAt.description: Watermark from when the caller reasoned about the target. The server detects writes since this id and applies `onStale`. SDK-managed; leave unset for last-writer-wins.

onStale.description: Conflict policy when `readAt` is stale. `reject` (default) fails the commit; `force` applies unconditionally. `flag`/`merge` are reserved.

AgentInput.description: Field payload for `agent` mutations. Send the full entity on CREATE (its `required` fields must be present); send only changed fields on UPDATE.

id.description: Server-assigned row id.

Agent.description: A `agent` record as returned by reads.

CommitOperation.description: A single typed mutation. The `model` field discriminates which `input` contract applies.

Datasource.description: A database connection registered for the caller's org. `connection` discriminates the kind; credentials never appear (no field exists for them).

id.description: Typed id: `ds_…` (direct, live), `ds_test_…` (direct, sandbox), `dse_…` (endpoint).

host.description: Direct kind only — safe to display.

schema.description: Direct kind only — Postgres schema of the synced tables.

endpoint.description: Endpoint kind only — the signed HTTPS endpoint URL.

key_last4.description: Endpoint kind only — last 4 chars of the signing key.

oneOf.title: Direct (connection string)
oneOf.description: Register your Postgres by connection string — what `Ablo({ databaseUrl })` calls. Sent once over TLS, stored sealed, never returned. The calling key's mode picks the environment (an `sk_test_` key registers your sandbox data plane).

connectionString.description: `postgres://` / `postgresql://` URL. Use a non-superuser, non-BYPASSRLS role; public hosts only.

schema.description: Postgres schema of the synced tables (default `public`).

oneOf.title: Signed endpoint
oneOf.description: Register a signed Data Source endpoint your app exposes — the credential never leaves your infrastructure. `environment` defaults to the calling key's mode.

signingKey.description: Write-only; stored by reference, never returned (only `key_last4`).

post.description: Submit a batch of typed mutations against a sync-group. Atomic: if any operation is denied or fails, the entire commit is rejected. Successful commits broadcast a typed `SyncDelta` to every co-present peer.

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Replay-safe identifier. Same key + same body returns the same receipt; same key + different body returns 409.

post.description: Mints a capability scoped to the requested sync-groups and operations. Returns the token only once — store it client-side. Mintable by human sessions and API-key principals; agents cannot self-mint.

get.description: Returns the metadata for a capability — does NOT return the token. Use to audit allowedSyncGroups/operations/expiry/revocation status.

404.description: Not found.

delete.description: Marks the capability as revoked. Future verifications fail with `capability_invalid`. In-flight WS connections holding this cap are not actively disconnected — TTL is the dominant constraint.

404.description: Not found.

get.description: Returns currently-held intents in the caller's org. Filter via query params.

post.description: Broadcast a pre-write declaration that the caller intends to act on a target. Other participants see the intent and can defer or coordinate. Idempotent on `intentId`.

delete.description: Releases the intent immediately. Idempotent — releasing an already-released intent returns 200.

get.description: Returns daily usage aggregates for the caller's org over the requested window. Group by `api_key_id`, `team_id`, `event_name`, or `line_item`.

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Replay-safe identifier; derive it from the business event, not a random value. Same key + same body returns the same receipt.

id.description: Client-chosen id on create (server generates one when omitted).

intent.description: Claim id this write is bound to.

readAt.description: Sync watermark the write was based on.

404.description: Not found or out of scope.

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Replay-safe identifier; derive it from the business event, not a random value. Same key + same body returns the same receipt.

post.description: Connect your database. The body's distinguishing field selects the kind: `connectionString` (direct) or `endpoint` + `signingKey` (signed endpoint). Requires a secret key; the org comes from the key, never the body.

201.description: The registered datasource (safe projection — no credential).

get.description: All of the org's datasources — both kinds, both environments.
One datasource by its typed id (status + operational health).

404.description: Error

post.description: Replace the signing key of an endpoint datasource in place. Direct datasources rotate by re-registering their connection string via `POST /v1/datasources`.

404.description: Error

tags.name: State
tags.description: Commits — write typed mutations to shared state.

tags.name: Capabilities
tags.description: Authority — mint, inspect, revoke capability tokens.

tags.name: Intents
tags.description: Coordination — pre-write declarations for multi-writer workflows.

tags.name: Tasks
tags.description: Audit envelope — group commits under a single agent run.

tags.name: Datasources
tags.description: Your database, connected to Ablo. One resource covers both connection kinds — `connection: 'direct'` (you hand Ablo a connection string; the `Ablo({ databaseUrl })` path) and `connection: 'endpoint'` (your app exposes a signed Data Source endpoint and keeps the credential). The org is always derived from the calling secret key; credentials are stored sealed and never returned.
